mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience with Kubernetes support for local development. It simplifies the setup process for running Kubernetes clusters on macOS, but may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource constraints and performance considerations.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the download page for Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

```

> Replace with the actual path if downloaded to a different location.
> Mount the downloaded DMG file.
> Copy Docker Desktop to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the DMG after installation.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop and Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
Launch Docker Desktop and enable Kubernetes support from the settings.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

```

> Open Docker Desktop.
> Open preferences after Docker starts.
> Enable Kubernetes in the settings.
> Apply changes and restart Docker.
commands: sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check if Kubernetes is running correctly on Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command should show the Kubernetes nodes.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This will remove Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

```

> Ensure Docker Desktop is not running.
> Remove Docker Desktop application.
> Remove Docker data and settings.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Backup your Docker images and settings, then upgrade Docker Desktop to the latest version.

```bash

docker save -o ~/docker_images_backup.tar $(docker images -q)

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all Docker images.
> Download the latest version.
> Open the downloaded DMG file.
> Mount the DMG file.
> Replace the existing Docker app.
> Unmount the DMG.
> Verify Kubernetes is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have started Kubernetes properly.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and check the Kubernetes settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl: command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'brew install kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes cluster may be overloaded or not responding.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation in Docker Desktop settings and increase if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** No Kubernetes resources have been created yet.

**Fix:**
Create a sample deployment to verify functionality.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and resource management.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum resource requirements for running Docker Desktop.
